seoTitle: Dean vs Deddick Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dean and Deddick Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dean vs Deddick Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dean and Deddick Valley.
heading: Dean vs Deddick Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dean, Victoria and Deddick Valley, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deddick Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Dean (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dean or Deddick Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dean has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Deddick Valley scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dean scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Deddick Valley scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dean or Deddick Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deddick Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
